problem.summary: Compact active graph vertices into a BFS frontier. The baseline uses a global atomic counter for every active flag. The optimized scaffold uses a block-local scan plus one global atomic per block, mirroring the custom surface behind hipCUB-style select/scan paths.
outputs: frontier contains vertices[i] for each active flags[i] in block-local stable order
frontier_count contains the number of selected vertices
constraints: optimized seed is stable within each block but block reservation order follows block index only because each block performs one atomic
frontier_count must be initialized to zero before launch
production BFS also needs duplicate suppression and visited marking

verification.method: TODO: compare selected set and count against CPU compaction and hipCUB DeviceSelect for stable ordering contracts.

curation.notes: Template-only BFS frontier compaction scaffold. The optimized path is a simple scan/select idea; hipCUB DeviceSelect remains the expected library baseline.
